blocks.subhead: The web home of the Forza franchise, for Turn 10 and Playground Games. Millions of players pass through it on their way to Motorsport and Horizon, so it had to work and look good doing it.
headline: Cars.
Community.
Creativity.

blocks.label: The brief
heading: Two series,
one home.
paragraphs: Forza is two flagship series and one very devoted community, so the site works as a franchise hub first. News, updates, car reveals and community programmes for Motorsport and Horizon alike, and it needed to feel like Forza before a single word is read.

blocks.text: Speed matters. The pages had to load quickly for a global audience, and the design had to feel like Forza - racing lines borrowed from the games themselves.

blocks.caption: An editorial system tuned for reveals, seasons and patch notes alike.

blocks.label: The system
heading: Editorial,
engineered.
paragraphs: The answer was an editorial system rather than a launch page. Modular components handle everything from cover reveals to patch notes, with the car photography doing the heavy lifting and the typography knowing when to step aside.
It had to hold up on a phone at a bus stop just as well as on an ultrawide, so the grid flexes generously and the type scale stays disciplined throughout.

blocks.label: In the wild
heading: Racing’s
front door.
paragraphs: The site serves one of racing’s biggest communities day to day, quietly flexing between seasons, launches and events. Of all the work at this scale, Forza is the one I still check in on regularly - the client has built on the system and their user base has expanded with it.
